items.headline: U.S. Equities Show Momentum as Q2 Earnings Season Commences
items.summary: U.S. equities are building momentum as markets reopen after the Independence Day holiday, with a generally bullish sentiment prevailing. Investors are keenly awaiting the Federal Reserve's June meeting minutes for clues on future interest rates. Despite ongoing concerns about AI spending and elevated tech valuations, the broader market remains resilient, driven by a rotation into other sectors. Oil prices are hovering near four-month lows, offering a measure of macroeconomic relief.

items.statContext: Up 0.38% from previous session. Futures were up 0.45% pre-market.

items.headline: AeroVironment Jumps on Army Contract Win
items.summary: AeroVironment Inc. surged after the defense technology company secured a $500 million contract from the U.S. Army.

items.headline: Apple Rises on iPhone Release Plans
items.summary: Apple Inc. saw a notable increase following reports of plans to introduce five new iPhone models.

items.headline: Netflix Advances Amidst Acquisition Speculation
items.summary: Netflix Inc.'s shares advanced after reports indicated the company is not bidding for NBCUniversal in the near future.

items.headline: HDFC Bank Boosts Indian Market Gains
items.summary: HDFC Bank drove gains in Indian stock markets, contributing to a strong positive close.

items.headline: Alphabet Dips After Antitrust Case Loss
items.summary: Alphabet Inc. shares fell after its Google unit lost a significant European antitrust case.

items.headline: Nvidia Under Pressure From Tech Rotation
items.summary: Nvidia slipped early in July as investors rotated profits out of tech stocks.

items.headline: SanDisk, Micron Fall on Supply Chain Concerns
items.summary: SanDisk and Micron Technology saw significant declines last week amid reports of Apple lobbying to source DRAM from restricted Chinese suppliers, signaling new competition in the supply chain.

items.headline: FOMC Minutes and Q2 Earnings Season in Focus This Week
items.summary: Investors are eagerly awaiting the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting minutes on Wednesday, July 8, which could provide crucial signals on the Fed's future interest rate policy. This comes after the Fed held rates at 3.50-3.75% in June, amidst a weaker-than-expected June nonfarm payrolls report, which added only 57,000 jobs. The Q2 2026 earnings season also kicks off this week with reports from major companies like PepsiCo ($PEP), Delta Air Lines ($DAL), Levi Strauss ($LEVI), and Hyatt Hotels ($H).

items.headline: ISM Services PMI Data Expected Today
items.summary: Today, market attention will shift to the U.S. with the release of the S&P Global Services PMI (forecast 51.4) and the ISM Non-Manufacturing Index (forecast 54.2, previously 54.5) for June. These reports are closely watched for insights into the health of the services sector, a major component of the U.S. economy.

items.headline: Sector Rotation Continues, Energy and Industrials Lead
items.summary: The market continues to see sector rotation, with investors moving profits from technology into blue-chip stocks and sectors with strong pricing power and steady demand, such as energy, industrials, materials, and utilities. Utilities are gaining particular attention due to the massive power demands of AI data centers. Conversely, the AI and semiconductor complex faced a correction last week.

items.headline: Oil Prices at Four-Month Lows, Gold Gains Safe-Haven Appeal
items.summary: Crude oil prices are hovering near four-month lows, easing inflation concerns and providing a macro relief to the markets. Meanwhile, gold has broken above $4,130, reinforcing its role as a safe-haven asset, while the 10-year Treasury yield, at 4.48%, continues to attract capital.
